codeReview.summary: The fix adequately addresses the original blocking issue. CLI flag overrides (--quality, --views, --audio-only, --format) are now validated immediately after being applied via the new applyAndValidateFlags() function, which calls validateFlagOverrides() before any download-related remote API calls (GetLectures, downloadLectures). The validation occurs after initClient() which performs login, but login is a prerequisite that doesn't depend on the validated flag values. Invalid values like --quality 1080 or --views sideways now fail with structured errors before reaching download operations.

summary: PASS - The fix correctly addresses the blocking validation bypass issue. The implementation adds validateFlagOverrides() which validates quality (144/450/720), views (first/second/both/left/right), and audioFormat (mp3/m4a/aac/opus) values immediately after CLI flag overrides are applied in executeDownload(). This occurs before GetLectures() and downloadLectures() remote calls, satisfying VAL-CLI-007 and VAL-CLI-011. The non-blocking views inconsistency is also resolved by accepting both canonical (first/second/both) and legacy (left/right/both) values in both config.Validate() and validateFlagOverrides(). Comprehensive tests cover all valid/invalid cases.
